summary: Hardware development, particularly CPU design, requires decades of accumulated experience and cannot be successfully executed by inexperienced teams, no matter how smart. Unlike software startups where young founders often succeed, every CPU startup that relied heavily on inexperienced engineers has failed, burning through hundreds of millions in funding. Physical work involves tacit knowledge that cannot be derived from first principles or learned from books alone, as illustrated by South Korea's multi-decade journey to build a shipbuilding industry despite access to expert consultants and detailed plans.
key_points: Successful CPU teams typically have core members who have worked together for decades, often spanning multiple companies
CPU startups funded with inexperienced teams universally fail, despite raising tens to hundreds of millions of dollars
Software allows for rapid iteration and real-time fixes, while hardware mistakes are extremely costly (minor bugs cost months and millions, fundamental flaws cost years and hundreds of millions)

Physical manufacturing requires tacit knowledge that cannot be learned from books or derived from first principles, as demonstrated by South Korea's failed attempt to build ships using Scottish expertise
Age discrimination doesn't exist in hardware design because experience is so critical - teams are typically composed of people in their 40s-60s
money_quotes: Every single such startup I know of failed
Physical work isn't the kind of thing you can derive from first principles, no matter how smart you are.
When the unpracticed Koreans tried to build ships using Scottish plans and detailed step-by-step directions, the result was two ship halves that didn't quite fit together and sunk when assembled.
With a large ship, 'minor' fixes can take days or weeks, and a fundamental flaw means that your ship sinks and you've lost half a year of work and tens of millions of dollars.
You need enough wisdom and experience to avoid common mistakes entirely – especially the ones that can't be fixed.
key_insight: The unforgiving nature of physical mistakes in hardware development makes experience indispensable, as there is no undo button and fundamental errors cannot be iteratively fixed like in software.
